title: Rootless podman: support passing arbitrary paths to configuration files
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

I'd like to be able to specify all configuration files to podman (rootless or not) via configuration flags or podman-specific environment variables.

On Podman 1.9.1 and 1.8.2, when I run `podman import result/tarball/nixos-system-x86_64-linux.tar.xz my-image`, I get:

> Error: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory

I found #1336 (rootless: allow to override policy.json by the user) but creating a file at `~/.config/containers/policy.json` doesn't seem to change the error. Additionally, since this is part of some automation, I do not want the runners to need to add configuration to their `~` either, and overriding `HOME` during the test phase is a bit ugly.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**


I'd like to be able to do this universally.

comments.body: We verified this isn't affected by containers.conf (1.8.2 still has the bug). Based on #1336 my first suspicion is some form of runtime config validation catching it, though it's possible that it could be somewhere in c/image as well.
This is definitely something in containers/image.  I don't believe Podman can currently override this.

Things like https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/6803e72bf87e737663db054d971135bc06d0747f/cmd/podman/images/pull.go#L84 exist, and c/image also allows specifying the policy path.

If `podman import` does not have the CLI option, and does not use the same rootless code to set the rootless overrides (which are _not_ c/image defaults), at least currently, that’s all entirely within the Podman repo.

I am getting the same error while i do `podman build .`
`STEP 1: FROM busybox
STEP 2: COPY sleep10.sh /home/
Error: error committing container for step {Env:[PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin] Command:copy Args:[sleep10.sh /home/] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:COPY sleep10.sh /home/ Original:COPY sleep10.sh /home/}: error obtaining default signature policy: open /etc/containers/policy.json: no such file or directory`
As I don't have root access, can't create the file.
Note : `docker pull` successfully runs.

correction: `podman pull` instead of `docker pull`

I have that file indeed. I am running rootless podman.
`podman run`, `podman pull` works fine.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I'm not sure this is stale.

Well we gave you two options, one is placing the policy.json file in your homedir.  Or using the command line option --signature-policy PATHTOPOLICY
What else do you need?

Basically if you install podman yourself, not using a distro package, then you need to create the appropriate config files.  If this is a distro package of Podman, then the disto package was not created correctly.

> If this is a distro package of Podman, then the disto package was not created correctly.

This is assuming the package manager is even able to create files in `/etc/` or `$HOME`.
Not all package managers allow this.

The flag `--signature-policy` seems very inconsistently exposed and is not documented in the `podman pull --help` output.

It's not available in `podman run`, which will attempt to pull an image if it's not locally accessible.

Primarily Podman needs to update c/image to include https://github.com/containers/image/pull/922 , for a global default; there hasn’t been a c/image release since. And then Podman would hopefully prune some of the Podman-specific-defaults code that sets the same defaults as that PR.

And then we _probably_ should add the explicit `--signature-policy` option for `podman import` and `podman build`, or wherever else it would make sense but it is currently missing.

Sorry for hijacking this one.
There are still issues with signature policy not being read from $HOME dir.
Is there another ticket to synchronize the behaviour for all podman commands?
Thanks
@QiWang19 PTAL. Let's focus on the policy parts.
